cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out the correct answer here. The question is about a study by the World Bank in 1974 regarding economic growth in some backward countries. So the key part here is "little benefit to a third of their population." That means that a third of the population didn't get much benefit, while the rest might have. But the answer options might not be based on that assumption. The question is about what the study concluded. Although GNP per capita did indeed rise, its benefit came down to only a small part of the population." So while GNP (growth) increased, the benefit was only to a small part of the population. The study in 1974 said that growth was of little benefit to a third of their population, but perhaps the benefit came to the rest.
